MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John McFadden

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did satisfy its burden of production, plaintiff created a triable fact on this issue with his declaration and deposition testimony and the declaration of Mr. Cohen. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Nov-23-2010 Off Calendar
